calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Derived from program_breakdowns matched to significant events and their beneficiary populations.

details.calculation: Programmatic spend was matched to reported interventions; costs were derived per matching beneficiary population.

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.elaboration: Quote: '17,244 TOTAL ANIMALS HELPED Besides the animals who were admitted into our hospital, we helped many others who were trapped, lost, or with minor injuries that could be treated directly in their neighbourhood by our Street Treatment team.'
details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 5%, Companion Animals: 55%, Unspecified: 39%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

significant_events.event_name: City-wide Catch, Neuter, Vaccinate, Release (CNVR) Program Launch

source.quote: At the start of the 2023, Animal Aid signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Udaipur Municipal CorporaVon giving Animal Aid the sole charge of running a city-wide CNVR program, ensuring humane treatment of the dogs and a scienVfic approach to managing the stray dog populaVon.

significant_events.summary: Launched a new city-wide CNVR program in January 2023 under an MoU with the Udaipur Municipal Corporation, giving Animal Aid sole charge.

significant_events.event_name: Reopening of Animal Birth Control Center

source.quote: A 120% increase compared to 2022-23, due to the reopening of our Animal Birth Control center and the launch of our dog sterilization program in January 2023, under an MoU with the local authorities.

significant_events.summary: Reopened the renovated sterilization center, which was shut down during the COVID pandemic, to intensify focus on prevention.

significant_events.event_name: Land Purchase for New Rescue Center

source.quote: In October 2023, we finalized the purchase of (almost) two more bighas, which provide us a strategic access to a side road and increased the total area of the new land to 8.3 hectares.

significant_events.summary: Finalized the purchase of two more bighas in October 2023, increasing the total area for the new rescue center to 8.3 hectares.

significant_events.summary: Major works completed in 2022 to refurbish the operating room and pre-surgery room, and construct a post-surgery recovery room.

data.original_text: Animal Aid Unlimited funds and provides honorary management to the Indian charity Animal Aid Charitable Trust (AACT) to relieve animal suffering in India through street animal rescue, medical treatment, sanctuary, and compassion education. They enable the Animal Aid hospital and rescue service in Udaipur, Rajasthan, India as the region's only hospital and rescue center for street animals. In 2020 alone, their helpline received 35,345 calls for rescue requests.

Our helpline received 33,237 calls for rescue requests, an average of 101 calls per day. We rescued 17,723 ownerless animals from Udaipur and nearby villages. We admitted 12,897 animals into our hospital for treatment: 10,211 dogs and puppies; 1,058 cows and bulls and 1,628 donkeys, cats and other animals. We vaccinated every animal admitted against rabies and other common diseases.
We completed 6,955 on site visits to provide medical treatment to animals in their neighbourhoods. We spayed / neutered 4,177 animals (a 50% increase compared to 2023.

Animal Aid Unlimited or AAU, founded in 2002, is an Indian animal rescue organization, based in Udaipur city of Rajasthan which rescues and treats animals that are sick, injured, stuck or in need of urgent medical aid and attention.
As of 2017, the organization claims to have saved more than 45,000 injured or ill dogs, cows, donkeys, birds and cats.
Our mission is to rescue and treat owner-less street animals of Udaipur who have become ill or injured, and through their rescue inspire the community to care for the lives of all animals.
Every year we rescue more than 15,000 injured animals in Udaipur, India.

Throughout India, animals including dogs, cows, cats and donkeys live on the streets. Most cities don't have hospitals for owner-less animals, which means injured or ill animals often die from treatable conditions. But in Udaipur, a call to our helpline saves a life.

We rescue street animals, provide medical care and sanctuary, and help turn community compassion into action. We combine on-the-ground, immediate relief to suffering animals with prevention to ensure long-term protection for animals.
Rescue Hospital and medical care Animal Birth Control Sanctuary Outreach and education Cruelty response.
Animal Aid Unlimited in Udaipur, Rajasthan — a non profit organisation which offers emergency medical treatment, care, rescue and sanctum to the local area's animals which are most in need.

At the start of the 2023, Animal Aid signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Udaipur Municipal CorporaVon giving Animal Aid the sole charge of running a city-wide CNVR program, ensuring humane treatment of the dogs and a scienVfic approach to managing the stray dog populaVon.

All during 2022, we did major works and refurbished our OperaVng Room and Pre- surgery room where paVents are prepped for surgery, as well as construcVng a post-surgery recovery room to accommodate a higher volume of surgeries each day.

Our outreach and education teams involve helpline callers in the recovery of the animals by providing regular medical updates, and giving thorough guidance about how to care for the animal once recovered and returned, to prevent future illness or injury. Our efforts result in animal lovers having an increased understanding of medical issues, when and how to help, and an even deeper bond with the animals around them.

While we have always spayed/neutered and vaccinated each one of our rescues before returning them back to the community, in 2023 we launched our new CNVR (catch, neuter, vaccinate, release) program with the reopening of our renovated sterilizaVon center, which we had to shut down during the COVID pandemic.
To reduce suffering, we have intensified our focus on prevention by re-opening our Sterilisation Center which we'd shut down during the COVID pandemic.
Since 2002 AAU has treated 50,000 street animals including dogs, donkeys, cows and bulls, from aged to orphans.
Because we treat many abandoned animals from the dairy sector, this experience provides opportunities to help victims of animal businesses (cows, bulls, working donkeys) in addition to many dogs.
The staff is all Indian and half are from tribal communities.

The charity's objective is the development of compassion and sensitivity in people of all ages, especially the young, through education about the needs of animals and ways in which they are exploited and made to suffer.
Every year marks not only the rehabilitation of thousands of animals, but incremental and lasting change in the community.
In 2024, they admitted 12,047 animals, including Dogs, cows, buffaloes, squirrels, goats, pigeons, cats.
Thousands of animals - who needed medical attention but did not need to be admitted- were treated directly in their neighbourhood.

ANIMALS STERILIZED: A 120% increase compared to 2022-23, due to the reopening of our Animal Birth Control center and the launch of our dog sterilization program in January 2023, under an MoU with the local authorities.

name: Animal Aid Unlimited & Animal Aid Charitable Trust
